title: PDF to CSV Converter — Free, Online, No Upload
description: Extract tables from text-based PDFs into CSV right in your browser. Free, no signup, your file never leaves your device. Best for digital PDFs with real text.
headline: PDF to CSV converter for text-based PDF tables
subhead: Pull tables out of digital PDFs into CSV — free, no signup, processed entirely in your browser.

presets.label: Upload a text-based PDF, preview the detected table, then download CSV. Works best on digital PDFs, not scans.

intro: This tool reads the text layer of a PDF and reconstructs rows and columns into a CSV file you can open in Excel, Google Sheets, or any spreadsheet app.

Everything runs locally in your browser — your PDF is never uploaded to a server. It works best on digital, text-based PDFs (like exported reports or invoices). Scanned or image-only PDFs have no text layer, so they cannot be converted here without OCR.

tutorial.title: How to convert PDF to CSV
steps: Upload a text-based PDF (one exported from a program, not a scan or photo).
The tool extracts the text and groups it into rows and columns by position.
Check the table preview to confirm the columns line up correctly.
Adjust the delimiter if needed (comma, semicolon, or tab).
Click "Download CSV" to save the extracted data to your device.

faq.question: Is my PDF uploaded to a server?
faq.answer: No. The PDF is read and parsed entirely inside your browser. Your file never leaves your device, which makes it safe for confidential documents.

faq.question: Why doesn't it work on my scanned PDF?
faq.answer: Scanned PDFs are just images with no text layer, so there is nothing to extract. This tool reads real text. Converting a scan would require OCR, which is not included here.

faq.question: The columns are not perfectly aligned. Why?
faq.answer: PDF has no concept of a real table — it only stores text at x/y positions. The tool guesses columns from spacing, which works well for clean digital tables but can struggle with complex or merged layouts.

faq.question: Can it handle multi-page PDFs?
faq.answer: Yes. All pages are processed and combined into a single CSV. Each page's rows are appended in order.

faq.question: What can I do with the CSV afterwards?
faq.answer: Open it in Excel, Google Sheets, or Numbers. You can also feed it into our CSV to PDF tool if you want a clean, reformatted PDF table.

disclaimer: Works only on text-based (digital) PDFs. Scanned or image-only PDFs cannot be converted without OCR. Conversion runs entirely in your browser — no file is uploaded.
